title: Burnham pulls back on suggestion to increase income tax allowance
description: The new prime minister previously said voters were frustrated over the freeze in tax-free personal allowance.
summary: The new prime minister has reconsidered a proposal to increase the income tax allowance, which was previously suggested to address voter frustration over the tax-free personal allowance freeze.

title: US renews strikes on Iran as Trump threatens to attack underground nuclear site
description: The US president's threat to target a key Iranian nuclear site came ahead of the 11th consecutive night of attacks.
summary: The US has renewed strikes on Iran, with President Trump threatening to attack an underground nuclear site, marking the 11th consecutive night of attacks. The move comes amid escalating tensions between the two nations. The exact target of the US strikes remains unclear.

title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
description: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.
summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky has dismissed the country's top army commander, Oleksandr Syrskyi, following days of protests over the removal of Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. The move comes as a response to the backlash against the decision. The situation has sparked controversy in Ukraine.

title: More women in their 20s are getting type 2 diabetes, experts warn
description: England is seeing "a worrying increase" in women in their 20s developing type 2 diabetes, say experts. Here's what to look out for.
summary: Experts warn of a rising trend in women in their 20s developing type 2 diabetes in England, citing a concerning increase in cases. The warning comes as a call to be aware of the signs and symptoms of the condition.

title: Ann Widdecombe was hit on head 21 times with hammer, prosecutors say
description: Joshua Kerry, 28, attacked Widdecombe while she was eating lunch at her home in Devon before stealing her wallet, prosecutors say.
summary: Former British politician Ann Widdecombe was allegedly attacked by a 28-year-old man who hit her on the head 21 times with a hammer, stole her wallet, and left her injured. The incident occurred at her home in Devon. The suspect, Joshua Kerry, is now facing prosecution for the crime.

title: 'I can't afford to turn the oven on': 7.4m households struggling to buy essentials
description: As Andy Burnham promises “breathing space” for households, millions are unable to buy items such as clothing, heating and food.
summary: Millions of households in the UK are struggling to afford basic essentials, with 7.4 million households unable to buy items such as clothing, heating, and food, amid promises of "breathing space" from Andy Burnham.

title: UK humanitarian worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital
description: The health worker was evacuated to the UK from the DR Congo after being potentially exposed to the virus.
summary: A UK humanitarian worker who was potentially exposed to Ebola in the DR Congo has been evacuated to a London hospital for monitoring. The worker was taken to the hospital for precautionary measures. The exact status of the worker's health is currently unknown.

title: Judge rules Trump must hand over records sought by BBC in defamation case
description: The judge grants a motion to compel the release of files held by the Donald J Trump Revocable Trust.
summary: A judge has ordered Donald Trump to hand over records sought by the BBC in a defamation case, as part of a motion to compel the release of files held by the Donald J Trump Revocable Trust. This decision is related to a defamation case, indicating a legal and political matter. The ruling will likely have implications for the case.

title: Tokyo urges men to wear shorts to work, but some women say it's 'leg hair harassment'
description: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government wants workers to ditch the suit and tie for t-shirts, trainers - and shorts.
summary: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government is encouraging men to wear casual clothing, including shorts, to work, but some women have expressed concerns that this may lead to unwanted attention and harassment. The move is part of a broader effort to promote a more relaxed work environment.

title: Great Apes kiss and cuddle to keep the peace, says study
description: Hugging before stressful events may be an ancient way of building trust, say scientists studying apes.
summary: A study on great apes suggests that they engage in kissing and cuddling to build trust and maintain peace, which may be an ancient behavior that has been passed down through generations. This behavior is observed before stressful events, indicating its potential role in reducing anxiety.
